relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus turns to crowds and disciples.
The teachers sit in Moses’ seat.
Instruction may be honored while hypocrisy is not imitated.
Heavy burdens are laid on others without help.
Visible religion can become performance.
Honor seats become a trap.
Public titles become spiritual currency.
The disciples are siblings under one Teacher.
Earthly titles must not replace the heavenly Father.
The Messiah is the true leader.
Greatness becomes service.
Self-exaltation falls; humility is lifted.
A gatekeeping spirit blocks others from heaven’s reign.
A later manuscript woe warns about exploiting widows beneath religious show.
Zeal without transformation can reproduce Gehenna-patterns.
Blind guides confuse gold and temple.
The holy source is greater than the object.
They confuse gift and altar.
The altar sanctifies the gift.
Oaths invoke more than they admit.
Temple speech touches the One dwelling there.
Heaven speech touches the throne and the One enthroned.
Tiny tithes cannot replace justice, mercy, and trust.
They strain a gnat and swallow a camel.
Outside cleansing cannot hide inside greed.
Clean the inside first.
Whitewashed beauty can hide tomb-death.
Outer righteousness can mask inner lawlessness.
Honoring dead prophets can coexist with resisting living truth.
They imagine themselves innocent of ancestral violence.
Their own posture witnesses against them.
The inherited measure is being filled.
Viper imagery exposes dangerous spiritual reproduction.
Sent messengers will again be resisted.
The bloodline of rejected righteousness is recalled.
This generation bears the crisis.
Jesus longs to gather Jerusalem like a hen under wings.
The house is left desolate.
Seeing him again waits for blessing the one who comes in the Lord’s name.
